title: 5 Daily Habits to Keep Your Heart Healthy
content: Maintaining a healthy heart doesn't require drastic lifestyle changes. Small, consistent habits — like a 30-minute walk, reducing sodium intake, and managing stress — can significantly lower your risk of cardiovascular disease. Start with one change at a time and build from there.

title: Understanding Malaria Prevention in the Rainy Season
content: With the rainy season approaching, it's crucial to take preventive measures against malaria. Use insecticide-treated nets, remove standing water near your home, and seek early treatment if you develop fever. Early diagnosis saves lives.

title: The Truth About Sunscreen: Myths vs Facts
content: Many people believe sunscreen is only needed on sunny days. In reality, UV rays penetrate clouds and can cause skin damage year-round. Use SPF 30+ daily, even when it's overcast. Reapply every 2 hours when outdoors.

title: When Should You See a Doctor for Headaches?
content: Most headaches are benign, but certain warning signs — sudden severe onset, headache with fever, or changes in vision — require urgent medical attention. Don't ignore persistent or worsening patterns. Keep a headache diary to share with your doctor.

title: How to Read Your ECG Report — A Patient's Guide
content: Understanding your ECG results can feel overwhelming. In this video, I walk you through the basic components of an electrocardiogram — what the waves mean, what a normal rhythm looks like, and which patterns warrant a follow-up visit. Knowledge is your best health tool.
